final_prompt.prompt:
Create an interior design rendering for a 20-square-meter Japanese wabi-sabi meditation tea room.

Style/medium: interior design visualization, wabi-sabi aesthetic, photorealistic architectural rendering
Composition: wide-angle interior shot capturing the full room layout, eye-level perspective, balanced negative space
Subject: low black walnut tea table with natural grain and fine cracks, several floor cushions (zabuton), irregular handmade terracotta floor tiles, rough clay flower vessel with dried branches and moss balls
Scene/backdrop: 20-square-meter meditation space, one wall in raw diatomaceous earth texture, opposite wall with floor-to-ceiling sliding shoji doors revealing bamboo grove silhouettes through translucent washi paper, skylight above
Lighting/mood: natural light streaming diagonally from skylight, soft diffused illumination, quiet and contemplative zen atmosphere
Color palette: off-white as base, natural wood tones as anchor, charcoal gray and moss green as accents, restrained and earthy harmony
Materials/textures: hand-fired irregular terracotta tiles with organic edges, black walnut wood grain with natural imperfections, rough diatomaceous earth wall surface, translucent washi paper, coarse ceramic vessel, natural moss
Constraints: must convey extreme restraint of Eastern aesthetics and inner tranquility; no modern furniture or bright colors
Avoid: glossy surfaces, perfect symmetry, bright artificial lighting, cluttered decorations, generic Scandinavian minimalism
